title: Function App Sample (Ingestion API)
descriptionMarkdown: The **Function App Sample** data connector demonstrates how to ingest custom JSON events into a Microsoft Sentinel custom table using an Azure Function and the [Azure Monitor Ingestion API](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/logs-ingestion-api-overview) (DCE/DCR). Use this as a starting template for building your own Azure Function–based connectors.

sampleQueries.query:
FunctionAppSample_CL
| where EventType == "Alert"
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: FunctionAppSample_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
FunctionAppSample_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
FunctionAppSample_CL
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(1d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Azure AD App Registration
customs.description: An Azure AD App Registration (service principal) is required. After the connector is deployed you will manually assign the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role on the Data Collection Rule (DCR) to this App Registration (see Step 1). [Learn more about registering an application](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app).

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** This connector uses Azure Functions and the Azure Monitor Ingestion API (DCE/DCR) to push sample events into Microsoft Sentinel. The ARM template creates the Data Collection Endpoint, custom log table (`FunctionAppSample_CL`), Data Collection Rule, Azure Key Vault, and Function App. Credentials are stored in Azure Key Vault by the deployment — the Function App reads them via Key Vault references with no plain-text secrets in application settings. **Two manual steps are required after deployment:** (1) assign **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** on the DCR (Step 3), and (2) ensure the deploying user has **Key Vault Secrets Officer** before deploying (Step 2). This might result in additional data ingestion costs. Check the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/) and [Azure Monitor pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/monitor/) for details.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 1 – Create an Azure AD App Registration
instructionSteps.description:
1. In the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com), navigate to **Azure Active Directory > App registrations > New registration**.
2. Provide a name (e.g. `FunctionApp-Sentinel-Connector`) and register.
3. Under **Certificates & secrets**, create a new client secret.
4. Note the **Tenant ID**, **Client ID**, and **Client Secret** — these are required in Step 2.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 2 – Deploy the Azure Function App
instructionSteps.description:
>**PREREQUISITE:** Before clicking Deploy, the account performing the deployment must have the **Key Vault Secrets Officer** role (or **Key Vault Administrator**) on the target resource group or subscription. This is required because the ARM template creates an Azure Key Vault and writes the `ClientSecret` into it as a secret. Without this role the deployment will fail with an authorization error on the Key Vault secret write.

Click **Deploy to Azure** and fill in the parameters. The template will create the Data Collection Endpoint, `FunctionAppSample_CL` table, Data Collection Rule, Azure Key Vault, and Function App.

[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Frobertmoriarty12%2FSentinel-Ingestion-Accelerator-Function-App%2Ffeature%2Fimplicit-hosting-plan%2FFunctionApp%2FData%2520Connectors%2Fazuredeploy_Connector_FunctionApp_FunctionApp_AzureFunction.json)

**Parameters to fill in:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `WorkspaceName` | Name of your Log Analytics / Sentinel workspace |
| `TenantId` | Azure AD Tenant ID from Step 1 |
| `ClientId` | App Registration Client ID from Step 1 |
| `ClientSecret` | App Registration Client Secret from Step 1 — stored in Azure Key Vault by the deployment |
| `AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID` | Full Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace (from **Log Analytics workspace > Properties > Resource ID**) |
| `FunctionAppLocation` | Azure region for the Function App and related resources. Can differ from the Resource Group location (e.g. set `centralus` even if your RG is in `westus`). Defaults to the Resource Group location if left blank. |

instructionSteps.title: STEP 3 – Assign Monitoring Metrics Publisher on the Data Collection Rule
instructionSteps.description:
After the deployment completes, grant the App Registration permission to ingest data:

1. In the Azure Portal, navigate to the **Data Collection Rule** created by the deployment (search by name in the resource group, or find it in the deployment outputs).
2. Go to **Access control (IAM) > Add role assignment**.
3. Select the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role.
4. Under **Members**, choose **User, group, or service principal** and select the App Registration created in Step 1.
5. Click **Review + assign**.

>**IMPORTANT:** This role must be assigned directly on the **Data Collection Rule** resource. Assigning it at the resource group or subscription scope is **not sufficient** — the Azure Monitor Ingestion API enforces the permission at the DCR scope, and a broader assignment will result in a `403 Forbidden` error. Wait 5–10 minutes for RBAC propagation before triggering the Function App.
